title: Author audio-quality spec v1 (LUFS / true-peak / noise floor / formats)
context: All read-aloud-audio narrations must meet consistent, objective audio quality standards before any human QA or publication. Without a versioned spec that defines concrete targets for integrated loudness (LUFS), true-peak ceiling (dBTP), noise floor, and acceptable output formats/sample rates, each donated recording will arrive with inconsistent levels, and the audio-mastering adapter will have no authoritative target. The spec must be established before narrate-001 and adapter-002 begin.
objective: Write and publish version 1 of the audio-quality spec that the audio-mastering adapter and all narrators/reviewers will enforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Specifies the integrated-loudness target (EBU R128 / ITU-R BS.1770) in LUFS, with the spoken-word distribution target and any host-specific variant (e.g. -16 LUFS vs -18/-23 LUFS).
Specifies the true-peak ceiling (≤ -1 dBTP) and the noise-floor threshold in dBFS or equivalent.
States that no clipping is acceptable and defines how clipped audio is handled (rejected, not silently passed).
Specifies the lossless master format (FLAC/WAV), sample rate, bit depth, and the derived distribution formats (Opus + MP3) with their target bitrates.
States that the audio-mastering adapter must emit a per-file quality report (LUFS, true-peak, noise floor, duration) before human QA.
Is published, versioned, and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
resources: PLAN.md (audio-quality bar and audio tooling sections)
output: A versioned audio-quality spec document (style-guide/audio-quality-spec.md), CC-BY-4.0, covering integrated-loudness target, true-peak ceiling, noise-floor threshold, clipping policy, lossless master and derived-format requirements, and the required per-file quality report fields.
